title: Anthropic makes Claude Fable 5 permanent amid competition
summary: Anthropic reversed its earlier decision and announced that Claude Fable 5 will be included in all Max and Team Premium plans starting July 20, at 50% of usage limits, and Pro and Team Standard users will receive a $100 credit. This change comes after competitive pressure from GPT-5.6 Sol and Kimi 3. This reversal demonstrates the intense market dynamics in the AI model space, where subscription plans must include top-tier models to remain competitive. Users of Anthropic&\#x27;s premium plans can now rely on continued access to the company&\#x27;s most capable model, alleviating concerns over the &\#x27;Fablepocalypse&\#x27;. The original plan to remove Fable 5 from subscriptions was driven by compute capacity constraints, and it remains excluded from the $20/month Pro plan. Max plans are priced at $100 and $200 per month, and the 50% limit means subscribers can use half of their total usage quota on Fable 5.
context: Claude Fable 5 is Anthropic&\#x27;s most capable widely released large language model, designed for demanding coding tasks and long-horizon autonomous work. It was originally released alongside a private version, Claude Mythos 5. The competitive landscape includes OpenAI&\#x27;s GPT-5.6 Sol, a flagship model with state-of-the-art coding and reasoning abilities, and Moonshot AI&\#x27;s Kimi 3 \(Kimi K3\), a 2.8 trillion parameter model that ranked third on benchmarks.

references.title: GPT‑5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
